      <FIELD>

        <FIELD_VALUE>Zsolt Szabasi</FIELD_VALUE>
      </FIELD>
      <FIELD>
        <NAME>Date</NAME>
        <FIELD_VALUE>2024-04-26 08:19:55+02:00</FIELD_VALUE>
      </FIELD>
      <FIELD>
        <NAME>Modification</NAME>
        <FIELD_VALUE>FDA0, FDA1, FDA2, FDA3, FDA4, FDA5, FDAB, FDAC:  data type encoding has to be ASCII instead of Hex.

FDB5 : data type encoding should be changed to"signed", the unit to "Nm", and the conversion formula should be filled 

EE04: data type unit should be changed to "ms"

Deactivate the unused DID reads in candela (EE00, EE40, EE41, EED0, , FD00, FD08, FD0A, FE00, EED1, FE10, FE11)</FIELD_VALUE>
      </FIELD>
      <FIELD>
        <NAME>Reason</NAME>
        <FIELD_VALUE>FORD_38_CEPS-18211</FIELD_VALUE>
      </FIELD>
      <FIELD>
        <NAME>SSDS Part Number</NAME>

      </FIELD>

      <SESSIONS_SUPPORTED>
        <SESSION ID="session_01">
          <NAME>defaultSession</NAME>
          <NUMBER>0x01</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>This diagnostic session enables the default diagnostic session in the server(s) and does not support any diagnostic application timeout handling provisions (e.g., TesterPresent service is not necessary to keep the session active). A server shall always start the default diagnostic session when powered up. If no other diagnostic session is started, then the default diagnostic session shall be running as long as the server is powered. </DESCRIPTION>
          <ENTRY_CONDITIONS>Vehicle Speed shall be less than 10 kmph</ENTRY_CONDITIONS>
          <EXIT_CONDITIONS>no specific conditions</EXIT_CONDITIONS>
        </SESSION>
        <SESSION ID="session_02">
          <NAME>programmingSession</NAME>
          <NUMBER>0x02</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>This diagnosticSession enables all diagnostic services required to support the memory programming of a server.  Regardless of whether the programmingSession runs in boot software or not, the ECU shall automatically suspend logging of DTCs and suspend transmission of non-diagnostic frames when entering the programmingSession.</DESCRIPTION>
          <ENTRY_CONDITIONS>Vehicle Speed shall be less than 10 kmph</ENTRY_CONDITIONS>
        </SESSION>
        <SESSION ID="session_03">
          <NAME>extendedDiagnosticSession</NAME>
          <NUMBER>0x03</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>This diagnosticSession is used to enable timed diagnostic service functionality (e.g., I/O control).  The diagnostic functionality in this session shall also include all supported diagnostic functionality supported in the defaultSession.</DESCRIPTION>
          <ENTRY_CONDITIONS>Vehicle Speed shall be less than 10 kmph</ENTRY_CONDITIONS>
        </SESSION>
        <SESSION ID="session_60">
          <NAME>tkpSession</NAME>
          <NUMBER>0x60</NUMBER>
        </SESSION>
      </SESSIONS_SUPPORTED>

      <SERVICES_SUPPORTED>
        <SERVICE ID="service_10" SESSION_REFS="session_01 session_02 session_03 session_60">
          <NAME>DiagnosticSessionControl</NAME>

          <DATA_DEFINITION>
            <DATA_TYPE>enumerated</DATA_TYPE>
            <ENUMERATED_PARAMETERS>
              <ENUM_MEMBER>
                <ENUM_VALUE>0x00</ENUM_VALUE>
                <DESCRIPTION>Validation Structure Verification Passed</DESCRIPTION>
              </ENUM_MEMBER>
              <ENUM_MEMBER>
                <ENUM_VALUE>0x01</ENUM_VALUE>
                <DESCRIPTION>Validation Structure Not Authentic</DESCRIPTION>
              </ENUM_MEMBER>
              <ENUM_MEMBER>
                <ENUM_VALUE>0x02</ENUM_VALUE>
                <DESCRIPTION>Validation Structure Invalid Format</DESCRIPTION>
              </ENUM_MEMBER>
              <ENUM_MEMBER>
                <ENUM_VALUE>0x03</ENUM_VALUE>
                <DESCRIPTION>Validation Structure Block Number Mismatch</DESCRIPTION>
              </ENUM_MEMBER>
              <ENUM_MEMBER>
                <ENUM_VALUE>0x04</ENUM_VALUE>
                <DESCRIPTION>Validation Structure Hash Value Mismatch</DESCRIPTION>
              </ENUM_MEMBER>
              <ENUM_MEMBER>
                <ENUM_VALUE>0x05</ENUM_VALUE>
                <DESCRIPTION>Validation Structure Memory Read Error</DESCRIPTION>
              </ENUM_MEMBER>
              <ENUM_MEMBER>
                <ENUM_VALUE>0x06</ENUM_VALUE>
                <DESCRIPTION>Validation Structure Block Mismatch</DESCRIPTION>
              </ENUM_MEMBER>
              <ENUM_MEMBER>
                <ENUM_VALUE>0xFF</ENUM_VALUE>
                <DESCRIPTION>Validation Application Routine Not Executed This Session</DESCRIPTION>
              </ENUM_MEMBER>
            </ENUMERATED_PARAMETERS>
          </DATA_DEFINITION>
        </SUB_FIELD>
      </DID>

      <DID ID="did_DE01">
        <NAME>Ford In House Software Feature Configuration</NAME>
        <NUMBER>0xDE01</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Write preconditions:

- ECU must be in Extended Diagnostic Session.

- Security Level 3 is required.</DESCRIPTION>
        <BYTE_SIZE>24</BYTE_SIZE>
        <DID_TYPE>packeted</DID_TYPE>
        <ACCESS_PARAMETERS>
          <READABLE SERVICE_REFS="service_22" SESSION_REFS="session_01 session_03 session_60" />
          <WRITEABLE SERVICE_REFS="service_2E" SESSION_REFS="session_03 session_60" SECURITY_REFS="security_level_2 security_level_3" />
        </ACCESS_PARAMETERS>

          <READABLE SERVICE_REFS="service_22" SESSION_REFS="session_01 session_03 session_60" />
          <WRITEABLE SERVICE_REFS="service_2E" SESSION_REFS="session_03 session_60" SECURITY_REFS="security_level_2 security_level_3" />
        </ACCESS_PARAMETERS>
        <SUB_FIELD ID="did_DE03_subfield_1">

          <DESCRIPTION>Configuration byte</DESCRIPTION>

DTC Enabled (Default)</DESCRIPTION>

      <DTC_STATUS_BITS_SUPPORTED>
        <DTC_STATUS_BIT ID="status_bit_7">
          <NAME>warningIndicatorRequested</NAME>
          <NUMBER>7</NUMBER>

        <DTC_STATUS_BIT ID="status_bit_6">
          <NAME>testNotCompletedThisOperationCycle</NAME>
          <NUMBER>6</NUMBER>

        <DTC_STATUS_BIT ID="status_bit_3">
          <NAME>confirmedDTC</NAME>
          <NUMBER>3</NUMBER>

      <DTC_FAILURE_TYPES_SUPPORTED>
        <DTC_FAILURE_TYPE ID="ftb_56">
          <NUMBER>0x56</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>Invalid / Incompatible Configuration</DESCRIPTION>
        </DTC_FAILURE_TYPE>
        <DTC_FAILURE_TYPE ID="ftb_49">
          <NUMBER>0x49</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>Internal Electronic Failure</DESCRIPTION>
        </DTC_FAILURE_TYPE>
        <DTC_FAILURE_TYPE ID="ftb_62">
          <NUMBER>0x62</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>Signal Compare Failure</DESCRIPTION>
        </DTC_FAILURE_TYPE>

          <NUMBER>0x4B</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>Over Temperature</DESCRIPTION>

      <DTC_SNAPSHOT_SUPPORTED>
        <DTC_SNAPSHOT_RECORD ID="snapshot_10">
          <NUMBER>0x10</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>The first time since DTC information was cleared or the DTC was aged that DTC status bit 3 (confirmedDTC) is set to 1. </DESCRIPTION>
        </DTC_SNAPSHOT_RECORD>
        <DTC_SNAPSHOT_RECORD ID="snapshot_11">
          <NUMBER>0x11</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>The last time DTC fault detection counter reaches its maximum value of +127.</DESCRIPTION>
        </DTC_SNAPSHOT_RECORD>
      </DTC_SNAPSHOT_SUPPORTED>
      <DTC_NUMBER_FREEZE_FRAMES_SUPPORTED>0</DTC_NUMBER_FREEZE_FRAMES_SUPPORTED>
      <DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORDS_SUPPORTED>
        <DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD ID="edr_02">
          <NAME>Operation cycle counter #2</NAME>
          <NUMBER>0x02</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>Number of operation cycles since the DTC fault detection counter last reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared).  The operation cycles during which the test was not completed shall be excluded</DESCRIPTION>
          <BYTE_SIZE>1</BYTE_SIZE>
          <SUB_FIELD ID="edr_02_subfield_1">
            <LEAST_SIG_BIT>0</LEAST_SIG_BIT>
            <MOST_SIG_BIT>7</MOST_SIG_BIT>
            <DATA_DEFINITION>
              <DATA_TYPE>unsigned</DATA_TYPE>
              <NUMERIC_PARAMETERS>
                <RESOLUTION numerator="1" denominator="1" />
                <OFFSET>0</OFFSET>
                <UNITS>Counts</UNITS>
              </NUMERIC_PARAMETERS>
            </DATA_DEFINITION>
          </SUB_FIELD>
        </DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD>
        <DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD ID="edr_10">
          <NAME>DTC Fault Detection Counter</NAME>
          <NUMBER>0x10</NUMBER>
          <DESCRIPTION>The purpose of this counter is to provide a mechanism for filtering the results of a low-level fault detection process so that test results (pass and fail) can be qualified before setting any DTC status bits. </DESCRIPTION>
          <BYTE_SIZE>1</BYTE_SIZE>
          <SUB_FIELD ID="edr_10_subfield_1">
            <LEAST_SIG_BIT>0</LEAST_SIG_BIT>
            <MOST_SIG_BIT>7</MOST_SIG_BIT>
            <DATA_DEFINITION>
              <DATA_TYPE>signed</DATA_TYPE>
              <NUMERIC_PARAMETERS>
                <RESOLUTION numerator="1" denominator="1" />
                <OFFSET>0</OFFSET>
                <UNITS>Counts</UNITS>
              </NUMERIC_PARAMETERS>
            </DATA_DEFINITION>
          </SUB_FIELD>
        </DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD>
      </DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORDS_SUPPORTED>
      <DTC_WARNING_INDICATORS_SUPPORTED>
        <DTC_WARNING_INDICATOR ID="warn_1">
          <NAME>PSCM Malfunction</NAME>
        </DTC_WARNING_INDICATOR>
      </DTC_WARNING_INDICATORS_SUPPORTED>
      <DTC_OPERATION_CYCLES_SUPPORTED>
        <DTC_OPERATION_CYCLE ID="op_cycle_1">Monitoring Cycle "A" - Ignition</DTC_OPERATION_CYCLE>
      </DTC_OPERATION_CYCLES_SUPPORTED>
      <DTC ID="dtc_5110">
        <NUMBER>0x5110</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Power steering Calibration Data</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_5110_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_56" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>Hardwired ignition is ON</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raised (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if the ECU detects incompatible tuning identification code.



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if the ECU detects compatible tuning identification code.</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>1. EPAS_Failure signal is set to POWER_STRG_ASSIST_FAULT (0x1) value

2. SteMdule_D_Stat signal is set to System_Failure (0x4) value

3. Fault will be active in the current ignition cycle and will be tested again at the following power cycle</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>1. Power steering assist is disabled for remaining ignition cycle

2. Driver will be noticed with the message "Power Steering Assist Fault"</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
            <SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
            </SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
            <EXTENDED_DATA_PARAMETERS EDR_REFS="edr_02 edr_10" />
            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service:

STEP 1  Check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2  If  testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP 2  Flash the correct original “As built data” on the ECU. Perform an ignition cycle. Go to STEP 3.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>
          </DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
          <DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>no</DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>NA</ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>
        </DTC_FAILURE_INFO>
      </DTC>
      <DTC ID="dtc_5B00">
        <NUMBER>0x5B00</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Steering Angle Sensor</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_5B00_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_49" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>Hardwired ignition is ON</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raised (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if the ECU detects steering angle sensor failure.



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if the ECU detects steering angle sensor failure is not present in the next power cycle.</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>The sent absolute position lost.   Absolute pinion angle (StePinComp_An_Est) go to 0.  And StePinCompAnEst_D_Qf go to No_Data_Exist.</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>1. All of the Steering Pinion Angle related functions are injured for the remaining of the power cycle.</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
            <SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
            </SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
            <EXTENDED_DATA_PARAMETERS EDR_REFS="edr_02 edr_10" />
            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service:

STEP 1  Check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2.  If testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP 2  Perform proper ignition cycle. Check if the DTC goes in history.  If testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 then go to STEP 3 else STEP 4.    

STEP 3  Lock to lock steering needed and straight driving above 20km/h until StePinCompAnEst_D_Qf switch to OK. Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If DTC is still present go to STEP 1.    

STEP 4  Most probably internal electronic failure. Replacement is needed</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>
          </DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
          <DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>no</DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>NA</ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>
        </DTC_FAILURE_INFO>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_5B00_fi_2">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_62" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>Hardwired ignition is ON</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raised (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if the ECU detects any of the following steering angle sensor related comparisong failure:

A) Relative Steering Pinion Angle (SteWhlRelInit2_An_Sns signal) gets faulty (this is a collector diagnostic, which activated if the signal can not fulfill the requirements, most case not the root cause)

B) Relative Steering Pinion Angle (SteWhlRelInit2_An_Sns signal) gets greater than rack travel



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if the ECU does not detect the steering angle sensor related comparisong failure next power cycle.</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>In the power cycle, when the failure is detected:

A) B) C) D) Relative Steering Pinion Angle (SteWhlRelInit2_An_Sns signal) and Relative Steering Pinion Angle Offset (SteWhlOSRelInit2An_Est signal) set to faulty value



One ignition cycle later, when the failure is not detected any more:

A) Relative Steering Pinion Angle (SteWhlRelInit2_An_Sns signal) and Relative Steering Pinion Angle Offset (SteWhlOSRelInit2An_Est) set to faulty value (not healed any more)

B) Relative Steering Pinion Angle (SteWhlRelInit2_An_Sns signal) and Relative Steering Pinion Angle Offset (SteWhlOSRelInit2An_Est) set to faulty value till steering wheel moved a little bit

C) Depends on the root cause existence.

D) Operation is normal</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>In the power cycle, when the failure is detected:

- All of the Steering Pinion Angle related functions are injured for the remaining of the power cycle.



One power cycle later, when the failure is not detected any more:

- All of the Steering Pinion Angle related functions are injured in case of A) failure criteria</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
            <SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
            </SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
            <EXTENDED_DATA_PARAMETERS EDR_REFS="edr_02 edr_10" />
            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service:

STEP 1  Check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to to STEP 2.  If testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 4.    

STEP 2  Perform proper ignition cycle. Check if the DTC goes in history.  If testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 then go to STEP 3 else STEP 7.    

STEP 3  Check assembled rack and configuration compatibility (DID DE00) if compatible go to STEP 4.  If not compatible go to STEP 6.  Check if the correct rack was built-in. If the correct rack is installed go the STEP 4. If not please do investigation on the rack side.  Check misalignment of the rack (asymmetric alignment and increased rack travel due to mechanical wear) if asymmetric alignment present go to STEP 5.

STEP 4  Lock to lock steering needed and straight driving above 20km/h at least for 30 Sec. Check StePinCompAnEst_D_Qf switch to OK than clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If DTC is still present go to STEP 1.

STEP 5  Set the proper alignment for the rack. Than go to STEP 4.    

STEP 6  Set the compatible value IN DID DE00 rack configuration. Re-flash the default EOL configuration of the vehicle. Perform a proper ignition cycle. Go to STEP 4    

STEP 7  Most probably internal electronic failure. Replacement is needed only if it’s stand alone and constantly active.</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>
          </DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
          <DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>no</DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>NA</ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>
        </DTC_FAILURE_INFO>
      </DTC>
      <DTC ID="dtc_600B">
        <NUMBER>0x600B</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Steering Shaft Torque Sensor 1</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_600B_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_49" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>Hardwired ignition is ON</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if the ECU detects steering torque sensor failure.



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if the ECU detects steering torque sensor failure is no longer present.</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>1. Assist will be disabled or highly reduced

2. EPAS_Failure signal is set to POWER_STRG_ASSIST_FAULT (0x1) value

3. SteMdule_D_Stat signal is set to System_Failure (0x4) value

4. Fault will be active in the current ignition cycle and will be tested again at the following ignition cycle</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>1. No power steering assist or highly reduced steering assist for remaining ignition cycle

2. Driver will be noticed with the message "Power Steering Assist Fault"</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
            <SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
            </SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
            <EXTENDED_DATA_PARAMETERS EDR_REFS="edr_02 edr_10" />
            <WARNING_INDICATORS_REQUESTED>
              <WARNING_INDICATOR WARN_REF="warn_1" />
            </WARNING_INDICATORS_REQUESTED>
            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service:

STEP 1  Check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2  If  testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP 2  Motor Sensor Failure. EPS Replaced Automatically by Service.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1.</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>
          </DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
          <DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>no</DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>NA</ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>
        </DTC_FAILURE_INFO>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_600B_fi_2">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_62" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>DTC is currently not used.</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>

      <DTC ID="dtc_600D">
        <NUMBER>0x600D</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Motor Rotation Angle Sensor </DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_600D_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_49" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>Hardwired ignition is ON</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if the ECU detects motor angle position sensor failure.



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if the ECU detects motor angle position sensor failure is no longer present.</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>1. Assist will be disabled or highly reduced

2. EPAS_Failure signal is set to POWER_STRG_ASSIST_FAULT (0x1) value

3. SteMdule_D_Stat signal is set to System_Failure (0x4) value

4. Fault will be active in the current ignition cycle and will be tested again at the following ignition cycle</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>1. No power steering assist for remaining ignition cycle

2. Driver will be noticed with the message "Power Steering Assist Fault". FIH features are not available.</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
            <SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">

      <DTC ID="dtc_9D23">
        <NUMBER>0x9D23</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Overheat Sensor</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_9D23_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_4B" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>If all of the following conditions are fulfilled:

1) Hardwired ignition is ON

2) Power steering assist is provided</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if the ECU measures overtemperature.



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if the ECU does not measures overtemperature any more.</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>1. EPAS_Failure signal is kept on EPAS_OK_NO_MESSAGE (0x0) value

2. SteMdule_D_Stat signal is set to Normal_Op_Limited_Assist (0x1) value</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>1. No power steering assist or highly reduced steering assist for remaining power cycle</FAULT_SYMPTOM>

      <DTC ID="dtc_C100">
        <NUMBER>0xC100</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Lost Communication With ECM/PCM "A"</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_C100_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_00" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>If all of the following conditions are fulfilled:

- battery voltage is higher than 10V

- Ignition_Status signal has "Run" (0x4)value

- PwPckTq_D_Stat signal has "PwPckOn_TqAvailable" (0x3) value

- EPS/PSCM's sent SteMdule_D_Stat signal does not show internal failure (0x4, 0x5, 0x6) values

- Hardwired ignition is ON

- 5100 milliseconds have been elapsed after once all the above condition met together</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raised (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if any of the following messages are missing:

AWD_4x4_Data 100ms

VehicleOperatingModes 5s

EngVehicleSpThrottle2 100ms

Torque_Status 100ms

EngVehicleSpThrottle 100ms

EngBrakeData 5s

PowertrainData_10 5s

TransGearData 5s

 

Pass Criteria:  This DTC goes inactive if all of DTC related messages arrives in time:

AWD_4x4_Data 

VehicleOperatingMode

EngVehicleSpThrottle2

Torque_Status 

EngVehicleSpThrottle 

EngBrakeData 

PowertrainData_10 

TransGearData </FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>It shall be signaled to FIH by setting qualifier bit and raise DTC.</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>Power steering assist activation and deactivation does not happen according the expectations, because the control signals are missing.</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
            <SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
            </SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
            <EXTENDED_DATA_PARAMETERS EDR_REFS="edr_02 edr_10" />
            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service:

STEP 1  Check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2  If  testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP 2  Check the cabling and the connectors of the EPAS module. If the connection is proper the origin of the problem is the ECM/PCM “A”.  Continue the investigation at the ECM/PCM “A” side. EPAS working as intended.  Additional hint: if it’s an individual DTC than check ECM/PCM “A” side, if more lost communication DTCs are active, issue is with the ECU connector of the EPAS most probably.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1.</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>
          </DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
          <DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>no</DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>NA</ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>
        </DTC_FAILURE_INFO>
      </DTC>

      <DTC ID="dtc_C121">
        <NUMBER>0xC121</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Lost Communication With Anti-Lock Brake System (ABS) Control Module "A"</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_C121_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_00" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>If all of the following conditions are fulfilled:

- battery voltage is higher than 10V

- Ignition_Status signal has "Run" (0x4)value

- PwPckTq_D_Stat signal has "PwPckOn_TqAvailable" (0x3) value

- EPS/PSCM's sent SteMdule_D_Stat signal does not show internal failure (0x4, 0x5, 0x6) values

- Hardwired ignition is ON

- 5100 milliseconds have been elapsed after once all the above condition met together</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:  This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) any of the following scenarios are met:  

  SCENARIO_A if all of the following conditions are met:  1) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: BrakeSysFeatures 

  SCENARIO_B if all of the following conditions are met:  1) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: BrakeSnData_2  

  SCENARIO_C if all of the following conditions are met:  1) Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction OR Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist OR Pull Drift Compensation functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs  2) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: BrakeSnData_3  

  SCENARIO_D if all of the following conditions are met:  1) Brake Pull Reduction functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs  2) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: BrakeSnData_4  

  SCENARIO_E if all of the following conditions are met:  1) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: WheelSpeed  

  SCENARIO_F if all of the following conditions are met:  1) Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction OR Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs  2) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: ABS_BrkBst_Data    

  SCENARIO_G if all of the following conditions are met:  1) Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction OR Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs  2) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: DesiredTorqBrk  

  SCENARIO_H if all of the following conditions are met:  1) Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs  2) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: WheelData    



Pass Criteria:  This DTC goes inactive if all of DTC related messages arrives in time: BrakeSysFeatures, BrakeSnData_2, BrakeSnData_3, BrakeSnData_4, WheelSpeed, WheelData, ABS_BrkBst_Data, DesiredTorqBrk</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>Default vehicle speed characteristics are activated in functions.

Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction OR Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist OR Pull Drift Compensation functionality is injured.  Power steering assist activation and deactivation does not happen according the expectations, because the control signals are missing.  Power steering assist is reduced.</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction OR Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist OR Pull Drift Compensation functionality is injured.

Power steering assist activation and deactivation does not happen according the expectations, because the control signals are missing.

Power steering assist is reduced.</FAULT_SYMPTOM>

              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>

                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />

            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service:

STEP 1  Check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2  If  testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP 2  Check the cabling and the connectors of the EPAS module. If the connection is proper the origin of the problem is the ABS.  Continue the investigation at the ABS side. EPAS working as intended.  Additional hint: if it’s an individual DTC than check ABS side, if more lost communication DTCs are active, issue is with the ECU connector of the EPAS most probably.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1.</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>

- EPS/PSCM's sent SteMdule_D_Stat signal does not show internal failure (0x4, 0x5, 0x6) values

- Hardwired ignition is ON

- 5100 milliseconds have been elapsed after once all the above condition met together</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Semi-Automatic Parallel Park OR Active Park Assist functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: ParkAid_Data_HS2



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if all of DTC related messages arrives in time: ParkAid_Data_HS2</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>FIH functional expert can describe the ECU's behavior.</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>Semi-Automatic Parallel Park OR Active Park Assist functionality is injured.</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
            <SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
            </SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
            <EXTENDED_DATA_PARAMETERS EDR_REFS="edr_02 edr_10" />
            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service:

STEP 1  check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2  If  testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP 2  Check the cabling and the connectors of the EPAS module. If the connection is proper the origin of the problem is the Parking Assist Control Module "A".  Continue the investigation at the Parking Assist Control Module "A" side. EPAS working as intended.  Additional hint: if it’s an individual DTC than check Parking Assist Control Module "A" side, if more lost communication DTCs are active, issue is with the ECU connector of the EPAS most probably.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1.</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>
          </DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
          <DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>no</DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>NA</ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>
        </DTC_FAILURE_INFO>
      </DTC>
      <DTC ID="dtc_C23A">
        <NUMBER>0xC23A</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Lost Communication With Image Processing Module A</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_C23A_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_00" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>If all of the following conditions are fulfilled:

- battery voltage is higher than 10V

- Ignition_Status signal has "Run" (0x4)value

- PwPckTq_D_Stat signal has "PwPckOn_TqAvailable" (0x3) value

- EPS/PSCM's sent SteMdule_D_Stat signal does not show internal failure (0x4, 0x5, 0x6) values

- Hardwired ignition is ON

- 5100 milliseconds have been elapsed after once all the above condition met together</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) Following message has timeout for more than 1000ms: Lane_Assist_Data1



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if all of DTC related messages arrives in time: Lane_Assist_Data1</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>It shall be signaled to FIH by setting qualifier bit and raise DTC.</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>The FIH features (LKA, LDW) are not available.</FAULT_SYMPTOM>

                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />

STEP 1  check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2  If  testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP 2  Check the cabling and the connectors of the EPAS module. If the connection is proper the origin of the problem is the Image Processing Control Module "A".  Continue the investigation at the Image Processing Control Module "A" side. EPAS working as intended.  Additional hint: if it’s an individual DTC than check Image Processing Control Module "A" side, if more lost communication DTCs are active, issue is with the ECU connector of the EPAS most probably.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1.</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>

            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>If all of the following conditions are fulfilled:

- battery voltage is higher than 10V

- Ignition_Status signal has "Run" (0x4)value

- PwPckTq_D_Stat signal has "PwPckOn_TqAvailable" (0x3) value

- EPS/PSCM's sent SteMdule_D_Stat signal does not show internal failure (0x4, 0x5, 0x6) values

- Hardwired ignition is ON

- 5100 milliseconds have been elapsed after once all the above condition met together</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) any of the following scenarios are met:

SCENARIO_A if all of the following conditions are met:

1) PSCM can not calculated reliable vehicle speed

SCENARIO_B if all of the following conditions are met:

1) CRC (checksum) OR alive counter problem detected on BrakeSysFeatures message

SCENARIO_C if all of the following conditions are met:

1) BrakeSnData_2 message's SteWhlComp_An_Est signal has Fault (0xFFF) value

SCENARIO_D if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction OR Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist OR Pull Drift Compensation functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) BrakeSnData_3 message's VehYawComp_W_Actl signal has NoDataExists (0xFFE) or Fault (0xFFF) value

SCENARIO_E if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction OR Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist OR Pull Drift Compensation functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) BrakeSnData_3 message's VehLatComp_A_Actl signal has NoDataExists (0x3FE) or Fault (0x3FF) value

SCENARIO_F if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Brake Pull Reduction functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) BrakeSnData_4 message's VehLatComp_A_Actl signal has Unknown (0x1FFE) or Fault (0x1FFF) value

SCENARIO_G if all of the following conditions are met:

1) WheelSpeed message's WhlFl_W_Meas OR WhlFl_W_Meas OR WhlFr_W_Meas OR WhlRl_W_Meas signal has Unknown (0x7FFE) or Fault (0x7FFF) value

SCENARIO_H if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction OR Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) ABS_BrkBst_Data message's YawStabilityIndex signal has NoDataExists (0x1FE) or Fault (0x1FF) value



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if all of DTC related messages arrives with correct value: BrakeSysFeatures, BrakeSnData_2, BrakeSnData_3, BrakeSnData_4, WheelSpeed, ABS_BrkBst_Data</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>Default vehicle speed characteristics are activated in functions.</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>Heavier, simple (vehicle speed independent) steering feel. The FIH features (LKA, LDW, Autopark, Drive modes etc.) are not available.</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
            <SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
            </SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
            <EXTENDED_DATA_PARAMETERS EDR_REFS="edr_02 edr_10" />
            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service: 

STEP 1  check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2  If  testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP 2  Continue the investigation at the Anti-Lock Brake System (ABS) Control Module side. EPAS working as intended.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1.



Note for operator:  

U0415-00 needs to be masked within PSCM at End of Line during Dynamic and Static in Ford assembly plants due to ABS module transmitting NO DATA EXISTS on VehLatComp_A_Actl and VehYawComp_W_Actl signals during the ABS self-test in Dynamic.  

An ignition cycle is required to move DTCs to history status in order to clear them using the clear command in the DET.  

Active PSCM DTCs cannot be cleared in the current ignition cycle. 

To prevent failures at gate code check, Vehicle Operations should perform an ignition cycle at End of Line between Dynamic and Static (after ABS exits Extended Diagnostic Session and before sending a clear command to PSCM).  

If this is not possible, U0415-00 needs to be masked when in history status at gate code check.</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>
          </DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
          <DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>no</DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>NA</ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>
        </DTC_FAILURE_INFO>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_C415_fi_2">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_22" />

            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria: 

This DTC is raised (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if the ECU detects: Signal value is over max value 

   

Pass Criteria:  

This DTC goes inactive if the ECU detects failure is not present in the next power cycle.    

PIF_SEL_ERROR  

VSPC_VEHICLESPEEDVALID_FALSE  

COMW_SG_BRAKESYSFEATURES_CRC  

COMW_SG_BRAKESYSFEATURES_ALIVE

SIGSTAT_SDMCHASSIS_VALID_FALSE  

COMW_SG_BRAKESNDATA6_CHECKSUM  

COMW_SG_WHEELSPEED_INVALID  

COMW_CS_BRKTOTTQACTL_INVALID  

COMW_CS_YAWSTABILITYINDEX_INVALID  

COMW_SG_BRAKESNDATA6_INVALID  

COMW_SG_BRAKESNDATA3_INVALID</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>Assist is provided based on safe speed (predefined) characteristic.  In case the vehicle speed is not available, it shall be signaled to FIH by setting qualifier bit, and assist provision shall use safe speed characteristic.</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>Heavier, simple (vehicle speed independent) steering feel. The FIH features (LKA, LDW, Autopark, etc.) are not available.</FAULT_SYMPTOM>

STEP 2  Continue the investigation at the Anti-Lock Brake System (ABS) Control Module side. EPAS working as intended.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1.</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>

      <DTC ID="dtc_C447">
        <NUMBER>0xC447</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Invalid Data Received From Serial Data Gateway Module "A"</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_C447_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_00" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>power on and ignition on</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>20ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) any of the following scenarios are met:

SCENARIO_A if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: VehicleOperatingModes_HS2

SCENARIO_B if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) Following message has timeout for more than 1000ms: EnVehicleSpThrottle2_HS2



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if all of DTC related messages arrives in time: VehicleOperatingModes_HS2, EnVehicleSpThrottle2_HS2</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>

      <DTC ID="dtc_C45A">
        <NUMBER>0xC45A</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Invalid Data Received From Parking Assist Control Module "A"</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_C45A_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_00" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>If all of the following conditions are fulfilled:

- battery voltage is higher than 10V

- Ignition_Status signal has "Run" (0x4)value

- PwPckTq_D_Stat signal has "PwPckOn_TqAvailable" (0x3) value

- EPS/PSCM's sent SteMdule_D_Stat signal does not show internal failure (0x4, 0x5, 0x6) values

- Hardwired ignition is ON

- 5100 milliseconds have been elapsed after once all the above condition met together</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Semi-Automatic Parallel Park OR Active Park Assist functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) ParkAid_Data_HS2 message's ExtSteeringAngleReq2 signal has Unknown (0x7FFE) or Invalid (0x7FFF) value



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if all of DTC related messages arrives with correct value: ParkAid_Data_HS2</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>It shall be signaled to FIH by setting qualifier bit and raise DTC.</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>The FIH features (Autopark) are not available</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
            <SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
            </SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
            <EXTENDED_DATA_PARAMETERS EDR_REFS="edr_02 edr_10" />
            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service:

STEP 1  check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2  If  testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP 2  Continue the investigation at the Parking Assist Control Module "A" side. EPAS working as intended.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1.</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>
          </DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
          <DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>no</DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>NA</ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>
        </DTC_FAILURE_INFO>
      </DTC>
      <DTC ID="dtc_C53B">
        <NUMBER>0xC53B</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Invalid Data Received From Image Processing Module A</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_C53B_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_00" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>If all of the following conditions are fulfilled:

- battery voltage is higher than 10V

- Ignition_Status signal has "Run" (0x4)value

- PwPckTq_D_Stat signal has "PwPckOn_TqAvailable" (0x3) value

- EPS/PSCM's sent SteMdule_D_Stat signal does not show internal failure (0x4, 0x5, 0x6) values

- Hardwired ignition is ON

- 5100 milliseconds have been elapsed after once all the above condition met together</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) any of the following scenarios are met:

SCENARIO_A if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) Lane_Assist_Data1 message's LaCurvature_No_Calc signal has Fault (0xFFF) value

SCENARIO_B if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Lane Departure Warning OR Lane Keeping Assist functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) Lane_Assist_Data1 message's LaRefAng_No_Req signal has Fault (0xFFF) value



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if all of DTC related messages arrives with correct value: Lane_Assist_Data1</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>It shall be signaled to FIH by setting qualifier bit and raise DTC.</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>The FIH features (LKA, LDW) are not available.</FAULT_SYMPTOM>

                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
            </SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
            <EXTENDED_DATA_PARAMETERS EDR_REFS="edr_02 edr_10" />
            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service:

STEP 1  Check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2  If  testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP 2  Continue the investigation at the Image Processing Control Module "A" side. EPAS working as intended.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>
          </DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
          <DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>no</DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>

        <NUMBER>0xE016</NUMBER>

        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_E016_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_09" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>power on</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>

            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) any of the following scenarios are met:

SCENARIO_A if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: VehicleOperatingModes_HS2

SCENARIO_B if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) Following message has timeout for more than 1000ms: EnVehicleSpThrottle2_HS2



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if all of DTC related messages arrives in time: VehicleOperatingModes_HS2, EnVehicleSpThrottle2_HS2</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>EPAS failure limp home

</FAULT_SYMPTOM>

          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>

            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />

            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if the ECU's SW or HW watchdog detects failure.



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if the ECU's SW or HW watchdog does not detect failure any more.</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>1. Assist will be disabled or highly reduced

2. EPAS_Failure signal is set to POWER_STRG_ASSIST_FAULT (0x1) value

3. SteMdule_D_Stat signal is set to System_Failure (0x4) value

4. Fault will be active in the current ignition cycle and will be tested again at the following ignition cycle</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>1. No power steering assist or highly reduced steering assist for remaining ignition cycle

2. Driver will be noticed with the message "Power Steering Assist Fault"</FAULT_SYMPTOM>

            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if the ECU detects not zero value on ANC_Diagnostic_Status or ANC_Diagnostic_Status. 

Or PSCM is detecting high CPU load during the CMA key update. 

Or PSCM is detecting related for runtime and other exception related error.





Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if the ECU detects zero value on ANC_Diagnostic_Status and ANC_Diagnostic_Status.</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>

      <DTC ID="dtc_E100">
        <NUMBER>0xE100</NUMBER>
        <DESCRIPTION>Initial Configuration Not Complete</DESCRIPTION>
        <DTC_FAILURE_INFO ID="dtc_E100_fi_1">
          <DTC_FTB FAILURE_REF="ftb_00" />
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>yes</DTC_CONTINUOUS_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
            <OPERATION_CYCLE OP_CYCLE_REF="op_cycle_1" />
            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>Hardwired ignition is ON</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) if the ECU detects an incomplete manufacturing configuration (CAL parameter file is missing, ECU Configuration DIDs have been not executed).



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if the ECU detects complete manufacturing configuration.</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>1. EPAS_Failure signal is set to POWER_STRG_ASSIST_FAULT (0x1) value

2. SteMdule_D_Stat signal is set to System_Failure (0x4) value

3. Fault will be active in the current ignition cycle and will be tested again at the following ignition cycle</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>1. Driver will be noticed with the message "Power Steering Assist Fault"</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
            <SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_10">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
              <SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA SNAPSHOT_REF="snapshot_11">
                <DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT DID_REFS="did_301F did_3020 did_330C did_3B4B did_D111 did_D117 did_D118 did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD09 did_EE03 did_EE04 did_EE05 did_EE06 did_EE42 did_EED1 did_EE07 did_EE43 did_DD05 did_EE90 did_FD00 did_FE10 did_FE11" />
                <SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>1</SAMPLES_SNAPSHOT_DATA_PER_RECORD>
              </SNAPSHOT_RECORD_DATA>
            </SNAPSHOT_PARAMETERS>
            <EXTENDED_DATA_PARAMETERS EDR_REFS="edr_02 edr_10" />
            <SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>Note for service:

STEP 1  check if testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 1 go to STEP 2  If  testFailedThisMonitoringCycle = 0 go to STEP 3.    

STEP2  Check the PSCM ECU configuration. If not OK, please configure it properly. Flash the correct original “As built data” on the ECU. Perform an ignition cycle. Go to STEP 3.    

STEP 3  Clear DTC and perform a proper ignition cycle. DTC should not present.  If present go to STEP 1.</SPECIAL_CONSIDERATIONS>
          </DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS>
          <DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>no</DTC_ROUTINE_MONITORING_SUPPORTED>
          <ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>NA</ECU_CONNECTOR_PIN>
        </DTC_FAILURE_INFO>

            <TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>power on</TEST_RUN_CRITERIA>
            <TEST_PERIOD>2ms</TEST_PERIOD>
            <AGING_COUNTER EDR_REF="edr_02" />
            <AGED_COUNT STATUS_BIT_REF="status_bit_3">40</AGED_COUNT>
            <FDC_INC_VALUE>1</FDC_INC_VALUE>
            <FDC_DEC_VALUE>1</FDC_DEC_VALUE>
            <FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>Fail Criteria:

This DTC is raies (testFailedThisMonitoringCycle=1) any of the following scenarios are met:

SCENARIO_A if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Following message has timeout for more than 100ms: VehicleOperatingModes_HS2

SCENARIO_B if all of the following conditions are met:

1) Torque Steer Compensation OR Brake Pull Reduction functionality is activated via ECU Configuration DIDs

2) Following message has timeout for more than 1000ms: EnVehicleSpThrottle2_HS2

SCENARIO_C: If one of the following conditions are met.

1) message GGCC_Config_Mgmn_ID_1_FD1 (0x40) is missed or

2) Signal: VehicleGGCCData is out of Range (1800mm – 2300mm) or

3) no data has ever been received.

One ignition cycle is required to use the transmitted tirecircumference.



Pass Criteria:

This DTC goes inactive if all of DTC related messages arrives in time: VehicleOperatingModes_HS2, EnVehicleSpThrottle2_HS2</FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA>
            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>Raise DTC. Assist provision will be limited, safe speed assist is provided.</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>Normal operation safe speed assist.

</FAULT_SYMPTOM>

            <ECU_FAULT_ACTION>1. Assist will be disabled or highly reduced

2. EPAS_Failure signal is set to POWER_STRG_ASSIST_FAULT (0x1) value

4. Fault will be active in the current ignition cycle and will be tested again at the following ignition cycle</ECU_FAULT_ACTION>
            <FAULT_SYMPTOM>1. No power steering assist or highly reduced steering assist for remaining ignition cycle  2. Driver will be noticed with the message "Power Steering Assist Fault"</FAULT_SYMPTOM>
